primary_accounting_issue: Éligibilité, dans les comptes consolidés, d’une relation de couverture du risque de change sur des dividendes intragroupe lorsqu’une créance de dividende a été comptabilisée

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS: IFRS 9 is the governing hedge accounting standard for hedged items and intragroup foreign currency exposures, so overlapping IAS 39 hedge-accounting guidance is discarded.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low — IFRS 9 clearly governs hedge-accounting eligibility, while IAS 21 remains relevant only as supporting guidance on whether intragroup foreign exchange differences survive consolidation.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern which items can be hedged and the limited exception for intragroup foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.

supporting_authority.reason: This paragraph explains when foreign exchange differences on intragroup monetary items are not fully eliminated on consolidation, which constrains the IFRS 9 intragroup hedged-item analysis.

This interpretation clarifies the distinct hedge-accounting model for hedges of a net investment in a foreign operation, which is a separate top-level hedge model relevant for peer-approach identification.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting models available under IFRS for foreign currency risk
Intragroup exposure eligibility in consolidated financial statements

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS hedge accounting includes fair value hedges for recognised assets or liabilities, which is a peer top-level hedge model for a recognised foreign-currency receivable exposure.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS hedge accounting includes cash flow hedges for forecast transactions or variability in cash flows, making it a distinct peer top-level hedge model within the same issue space.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS separately recognises net investment hedges as a distinct hedge-accounting model for foreign currency risk, so it should be retained as a peer top-level approach.
